heroHeadline: Day 33: Argentina Designates CJNG Terrorist Organization; Succession Vacuum Enters Week 5
heroSubtitle: Day 33 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. Argentina joined the United States in formally designating CJNG a terrorist organization on March 26, citing the cartel's presence in 40+ countries and its expanding Latin American footprint. 'El 03' (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues consolidating CJNG leadership under a collective council structure, with DEA-SEDENA intelligence cooperation maintaining sustained pressure on the network. Rival organizations continue pressing CJNG's boundaries in Michoacán, Zacatecas, and Guanajuato as the post-Mencho succession vacuum enters its fifth week.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, UPI, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 27, 2026.
